basics.name: Torrey J. Mortenson, PhD
basics.summary: Human Factors Research Scientist at Idaho National Laboratory. Adjunct Professor at University of Idaho. Founder of Civix Design. Research interests in human reliability analysis, human-computer interaction, explainable AI, and cognitive modeling in safety-critical systems.

work.summary: Human-centered design consultancy focused on civic services and high-stakes public/private sector clients. Services include service design, digital design, ADA/WCAG accessibility compliance, AI integration and risk evaluation, and process design.

work.company: Idaho National Laboratory / Battelle Energy Alliance
work.summary: Research in human reliability analysis, human factors, interface development and design, and data visualization. Developing NRC guidance on advanced reactor operator licensing and remote/autonomous operations human factors.

Graduate and undergraduate instruction in human performance, safety, and human-computer interaction. Courses include PSYC561 (HCI), INDT466 (Human Performance Field Investigation), TM528 (Accident Investigation), and NE404 (Human Centered Design for Nuclear Systems).

work.position: Materials and Fuels Complex Performance Analyst
work.summary: Supported Post-Irradiation Examination and Advanced Characterization divisions on Contractor Assurance principles. Developed HPI tools and management review meeting templates. Cause analyst on emergent incidents.
